title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
At 26 Nutmeg Street, a residence for students, Lenn Bates and Valerie move through the shared living spaces. Mrs. Haberdash goes to visit Mrs. Nicolaitis in her heated living room. Mrs. Nicolaitis expresses outrage over rising food bills, accusing Mrs. Haberdash of being robbed or deceived by the Italian chef. Mrs. Haberdash defends the costs as necessary for nutritious meals and explains that she utilized discounts from Lumpson's. 

During their argument, Mrs. Nicolaitis expresses a desire to prevent Sally Finch, an American Fulbright scholar, from leaving the residence, though she admits she does not know the real reason for Sally's departure. Mrs. Nicolaitis also makes discriminatory remarks regarding non-white students and accuses Nigel Chapman of being a communist. Mrs. Haberdash defends the students and dismisses the accusations.

After leaving the confrontation, Mrs. Haberdash is approached by Elizabeth Johnston, a law student from the West Indies. Elizabeth leads Mrs. Haberdash to her top-floor room to reveal that her thesis has been ruined by spilled ink. Elizabeth notes that the ink is still wet and specifies that it is green ink, which is not her own. Elizabeth identifies Nigel Chapman as someone who uses green ink, though she expresses doubt that he is the culprit. Mrs. Haberdash promises to investigate the incident. Following this, Mrs. Haberdash heads toward the door of Sally Finch's room.
